label: Paged attention & KV-memory management

node.label: Paged attention & KV-memory management
node.definition: Paged attention manages key–value cache blocks through virtual-memory-like allocation so variable-length generations share accelerator memory efficiently.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Paged attention & KV-memory management is modeled as depending on Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling, KV cache & recurrent inference state and feeding downstream nodes such as Large language models, Conversational assistants & chatbots.
examples: atlas downstream link to Large language models
atlas downstream link to Conversational assistants & chatbots
limitations: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling, KV cache & recurrent inference state.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Paged attention & KV-memory management has an `operationalizes` dependency on Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling because Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Paged attention & KV-memory management has an `operationalizes` dependency on KV cache & recurrent inference state because KV cache & recurrent inference state is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.

In this atlas graph, Paged attention & KV-memory management has an `operationalizes` dependency on Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention because Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention is modeled as infrastructure needed to operationalize the node.
